name: CHURG-STRAUSS SYNDROME
definition: ALLERGIC ANGIITIS AND GRANULOMATOSIS = EOSINOPHILIC GRANULOMATOSIS WITH rare multisystemic disorder with necrotizing vasculitis of small vessels associated with

demographics.Etiology: ? hypersensitivity response to an inhaled agent

dominant_finding: subpleural consolidation / ground-glass attenuation in a lobular distribution (59%)
distribution: subpleural consolidation / ground-glass attenuation in a lobular distribution (59%)
discriminating_features: subpleural consolidation / ground-glass attenuation in a lobular distribution (59%)

HRCT: subpleural consolidation / ground-glass attenuation in a lobular distribution (59%)
Lung (most frequent): intraalveolar hemo: often transient peripheral widespread nonsegmental air-space opacities without zonal
nodules may coalesce up to 2 cm (rare)
GI tract (20%): ulceration, hemorrhage,: eosinophilic gastroenteritis (= bowel wall infiltration by eosinophils)
CNS (commonly involved): micro- or macrohemorrhages of cerebral ischemia

clinical.Prognosis: 85% 5-year survival; death from intraabdominal /cardiac complications, cerebral
